questions.text: Choose the word that best fits the blank: She is a _____ of diligence and integrity.
questions.solution:
Question word: **paragon** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** A perfect example of a quality; a model of excellence.
**Hindi Meaning:** प्रेष्ठ उदाहरण, आदर्श
**Synonyms:** Epitome, embodiment, quintessence, ideal, exemplar
**Antonyms:** Antithesis, flaw, deformity, imperfection
**Example:** She is a paragon of diligence and integrity.

**Option 0: flaw**
**English Meaning:** A defect or imperfection.
**Hindi Meaning:** दोष
**Synonyms:** Deformity, imperfection
**Antonyms:** Paragon, ideal
**Example:** There was a small flaw in the diamond.

**Option 1: deformity**
**English Meaning:** A distorted or abnormal shape.
**Hindi Meaning:** विकृति
**Synonyms:** Flaw, imperfection
**Antonyms:** Paragon
**Example:** The accident left him with a deformity.

**Option 2: paragon** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** A perfect example of a quality; a model of excellence.
**Hindi Meaning:** प्रेष्ठ उदाहरण, आदर्श
**Synonyms:** Epitome, embodiment, quintessence, ideal
**Antonyms:** Antithesis, flaw
**Example:** She is a paragon of diligence and integrity.

**Option 3: antithesis**
**English Meaning:** The direct opposite.
**Hindi Meaning:** विपरीत
**Synonyms:** Opposite
**Antonyms:** Paragon
**Example:** He is the antithesis of his brother.

**Option 4: imperfection**
**English Meaning:** A fault or defect.
**Hindi Meaning:** कमी
**Synonyms:** Flaw, deformity
**Antonyms:** Paragon
**Example:** No one is without imperfection.

The sentence praises someone as a perfect example. Only 'paragon' fits.

Question word: **Foment** (the correct synonym is 'Instigate')

**English Meaning:** To stir up, provoke, or instigate trouble or change.
**Hindi Meaning:** भड़काना, उकसाना
**Synonyms:** Instigate, incite, agitate, stir up, provoke
**Antonyms:** Suppress, quell, curb, pacify
**Example:** The extremist group tried to foment unrest among workers.

**Option 0: Suppress**
**English Meaning:** To put an end to something.
**Hindi Meaning:** दबाना
**Synonyms:** Quell, curb
**Antonyms:** Foment
**Example:** The government tried to suppress the news.

**Option 1: Quell**
**English Meaning:** To put an end to a rebellion.
**Hindi Meaning:** शांत करना
**Synonyms:** Suppress, pacify
**Antonyms:** Foment
**Example:** The police quelled the riot.

**Option 2: Instigate** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** To stir up, provoke, or instigate trouble or change.
**Hindi Meaning:** भड़काना, उकसाना
**Synonyms:** Incite, agitate
**Antonyms:** Suppress
**Example:** The extremist group tried to foment unrest among workers.

**Option 3: Curb**
**English Meaning:** To control or limit.
**Hindi Meaning:** नियंत्रित करना
**Synonyms:** Quell, suppress
**Antonyms:** Foment
**Example:** The new law will curb corruption.

**Option 4: Pacify**
**English Meaning:** To calm someone down.
**Hindi Meaning:** शांत करना
**Synonyms:** Quell, curb
**Antonyms:** Foment
**Example:** The teacher pacified the angry students.

The word means to stir up trouble. 'Instigate' is the closest synonym.

Question word: **Apogee**

**English Meaning:** The highest or most successful point; peak.
**Hindi Meaning:** चरम बिंदु, शीर्ष
**Synonyms:** Zenith, culmination, pinnacle, summit, apex
**Antonyms:** Nadir, base, bottom
**Example:** His Nobel Prize marked the apogee of a brilliant career.

**Option 0: Zenith**
**English Meaning:** The highest point.
**Hindi Meaning:** चरम सीमा
**Synonyms:** Culmination, pinnacle
**Antonyms:** Nadir
**Example:** The zenith of his career was the Nobel Prize.

**Option 1: Culmination**
**English Meaning:** The highest point of development.
**Hindi Meaning:** चरमोत्कर्ष
**Synonyms:** Zenith, pinnacle
**Antonyms:** Nadir
**Example:** The culmination of years of hard work.

**Option 2: Pinnacle**
**English Meaning:** The highest point of success.
**Hindi Meaning:** शिखर
**Synonyms:** Zenith, summit
**Antonyms:** Nadir
**Example:** The pinnacle of his achievement.

**Option 3: Nadir** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** The lowest point.
**Hindi Meaning:** निचला बिंदु
**Synonyms:** Base, bottom
**Antonyms:** Apogee, zenith
**Example:** His Nobel Prize marked the apogee of a brilliant career.

**Option 4: Summit**
**English Meaning:** The highest point.
**Hindi Meaning:** शिखर
**Synonyms:** Pinnacle, zenith
**Antonyms:** Nadir
**Example:** The summit of his career.

The question asks for the antonym. 'Nadir' (lowest point) is the opposite of 'Apogee' (highest point).

questions.text: Choose the word that best fits the blank: Bureaucratic red tape _____ every creative idea.
questions.solution:
Question word: **stultify** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** To make someone or something appear foolish; to impair or hinder development.
**Hindi Meaning:** मूर्खतापूर्ण बनाना, निष्क्रिय करना
**Synonyms:** Impair, stifle, undermine, suppress, dull
**Antonyms:** Inspire, strengthen, enhance, stimulate
**Example:** Bureaucratic red tape stultified every creative idea.

**Option 0: inspire**
**English Meaning:** To motivate or encourage.
**Hindi Meaning:** प्रेरित करना
**Synonyms:** Strengthen, stimulate
**Antonyms:** Stultify
**Example:** The speech inspired the students.

**Option 1: strengthen**
**English Meaning:** To make stronger.
**Hindi Meaning:** मजबूत करना
**Synonyms:** Enhance, stimulate
**Antonyms:** Stultify
**Example:** Exercise strengthens the body.

**Option 2: stultify** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** To make someone or something appear foolish; to impair or hinder development.
**Hindi Meaning:** मूर्खतापूर्ण बनाना, निष्क्रिय करना
**Synonyms:** Impair, stifle, undermine
**Antonyms:** Inspire, strengthen
**Example:** Bureaucratic red tape stultified every creative idea.

**Option 3: enhance**
**English Meaning:** To improve or increase.
**Hindi Meaning:** बढ़ाना
**Synonyms:** Strengthen, stimulate
**Antonyms:** Stultify
**Example:** The new software will enhance productivity.

**Option 4: stimulate**
**English Meaning:** To encourage activity.
**Hindi Meaning:** उत्तेजित करना
**Synonyms:** Inspire, strengthen
**Antonyms:** Stultify
**Example:** Coffee can stimulate the brain.

The sentence shows something that stops creative ideas. Only 'stultify' fits.

Question word: **Mendicant** (the correct synonym is 'Beggar')

**English Meaning:** A beggar; someone who lives by asking for alms.
**Hindi Meaning:** भिखारी, याचक
**Synonyms:** Beggar, pauper, panhandler, vagrant
**Antonyms:** Magnate, aristocrat, benefactor
**Example:** The city’s temples attract both tourists and mendicants.

**Option 0: Magnate**
**English Meaning:** A wealthy and powerful person.
**Hindi Meaning:** धनाढ्य व्यक्ति
**Synonyms:** Aristocrat, benefactor
**Antonyms:** Mendicant
**Example:** He is a business magnate.

**Option 1: Aristocrat**
**English Meaning:** A member of the nobility.
**Hindi Meaning:** कुलीन
**Synonyms:** Magnate
**Antonyms:** Mendicant
**Example:** He belongs to an aristocratic family.

**Option 2: Beggar** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** A beggar; someone who lives by asking for alms.
**Hindi Meaning:** भिखारी, याचक
**Synonyms:** Pauper, panhandler
**Antonyms:** Magnate, aristocrat
**Example:** The city’s temples attract both tourists and mendicants.

**Option 3: Benefactor**
**English Meaning:** A person who gives help or money.
**Hindi Meaning:** दाता
**Synonyms:** Philanthropist
**Antonyms:** Mendicant
**Example:** He is a great benefactor of the school.

**Option 4: Philanthropist**
**English Meaning:** A person who donates to good causes.
**Hindi Meaning:** परोपकारी
**Synonyms:** Benefactor
**Antonyms:** Mendicant
**Example:** He is a well-known philanthropist.

The word means a beggar. 'Beggar' is the closest synonym.

Question word: **Quiddity**

**English Meaning:** The essential nature or distinctive feature of something.
**Hindi Meaning:** सार, मूल तत्व
**Synonyms:** Essence, core, intrinsic nature, quintessence
**Antonyms:** Superficiality, exaggeration, adornment
**Example:** The quiddity of democracy lies in public accountability.

**Option 0: Essence**
**English Meaning:** The basic nature.
**Hindi Meaning:** सार
**Synonyms:** Core, quintessence
**Antonyms:** Superficiality
**Example:** The essence of the story is love.

**Option 1: Core**
**English Meaning:** The central part.
**Hindi Meaning:** मूल
**Synonyms:** Essence
**Antonyms:** Superficiality
**Example:** The core of the problem is funding.

**Option 2: Superficiality** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Lack of depth; surface-level.
**Hindi Meaning:** सतहीपन
**Synonyms:** Exaggeration, adornment
**Antonyms:** Quiddity, essence
**Example:** The quiddity of democracy lies in public accountability.

**Option 3: Intrinsic nature**
**English Meaning:** The essential quality.
**Hindi Meaning:** अंतर्निहित स्वभाव
**Synonyms:** Essence, core
**Antonyms:** Superficiality
**Example:** The intrinsic nature of the material.

**Option 4: Quintessence**
**English Meaning:** The perfect example of a quality.
**Hindi Meaning:** सारतत्व
**Synonyms:** Essence
**Antonyms:** Superficiality
**Example:** She is the quintessence of beauty.

The question asks for the antonym. 'Superficiality' (surface-level) is the opposite of 'Quiddity' (essential nature).

questions.text: Choose the word that best fits the blank: His _____ handling of the crisis cost the party its reputation.
questions.solution:
Question word: **maladroit** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** Clumsy, unskillful, or awkward.
**Hindi Meaning:** अनाड़ी, अयोग्य
**Synonyms:** Clumsy, bungling, inept, awkward
**Antonyms:** Skillful, dexterous, adroit, competent
**Example:** His maladroit handling of the crisis cost the party its reputation.

**Option 0: skillful**
**English Meaning:** Having or showing skill.
**Hindi Meaning:** कुशल
**Synonyms:** Dexterous, adroit
**Antonyms:** Maladroit
**Example:** She is a skillful dancer.

**Option 1: dexterous**
**English Meaning:** Skillful with hands.
**Hindi Meaning:** निपुण
**Synonyms:** Skillful, adroit
**Antonyms:** Maladroit
**Example:** He is dexterous with tools.

**Option 2: maladroit** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Clumsy, unskillful, or awkward.
**Hindi Meaning:** अनाड़ी, अयोग्य
**Synonyms:** Clumsy, bungling, inept
**Antonyms:** Skillful, dexterous
**Example:** His maladroit handling of the crisis cost the party its reputation.

**Option 3: adroit**
**English Meaning:** Clever or skillful.
**Hindi Meaning:** चतुर
**Synonyms:** Dexterous, skillful
**Antonyms:** Maladroit
**Example:** Her adroit reply impressed everyone.

**Option 4: competent**
**English Meaning:** Having the necessary ability.
**Hindi Meaning:** सक्षम
**Synonyms:** Skillful
**Antonyms:** Maladroit
**Example:** He is competent in his job.

The sentence shows clumsy handling that caused damage. Only 'maladroit' fits.

Question word: **Disapprobation** (the correct synonym is 'Condemnation')

**English Meaning:** Strong disapproval; condemnation.
**Hindi Meaning:** अस्वीकृति, निंदा
**Synonyms:** Condemnation, censure, reproach, criticism
**Antonyms:** Sanction, endorsement, commendation, approval
**Example:** The film faced disapprobation from conservative groups.

**Option 0: Sanction**
**English Meaning:** Official approval.
**Hindi Meaning:** मंजूरी
**Synonyms:** Endorsement, approval
**Antonyms:** Disapprobation
**Example:** The project received government sanction.

**Option 1: Endorsement**
**English Meaning:** Support or approval.
**Hindi Meaning:** समर्थन
**Synonyms:** Sanction, approval
**Antonyms:** Disapprobation
**Example:** The celebrity gave endorsement to the brand.

**Option 2: Condemnation** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Strong disapproval; condemnation.
**Hindi Meaning:** अस्वीकृति, निंदा
**Synonyms:** Censure, reproach
**Antonyms:** Sanction, endorsement
**Example:** The film faced disapprobation from conservative groups.

**Option 3: Commendation**
**English Meaning:** Praise or approval.
**Hindi Meaning:** प्रशंसा
**Synonyms:** Approval
**Antonyms:** Disapprobation
**Example:** He received commendation for his work.

**Option 4: Approval**
**English Meaning:** Agreement or permission.
**Hindi Meaning:** अनुमोदन
**Synonyms:** Sanction, endorsement
**Antonyms:** Disapprobation
**Example:** The plan got quick approval.

The word means strong disapproval. 'Condemnation' is the closest synonym.

Question word: **Ineluctability**

**English Meaning:** The quality of being unavoidable; something that cannot be escaped.
**Hindi Meaning:** अनिवार्यता, अपरिहार्यता
**Synonyms:** Certainty, inevitability, fatality
**Antonyms:** Possibility, prevention, avoidability
**Example:** The ineluctability of death is a core theme of Stoic philosophy.

**Option 0: Certainty**
**English Meaning:** The state of being sure.
**Hindi Meaning:** निश्चितता
**Synonyms:** Inevitability
**Antonyms:** Avoidability
**Example:** The certainty of the result was clear.

**Option 1: Inevitability**
**English Meaning:** The fact that something is certain to happen.
**Hindi Meaning:** अनिवार्यता
**Synonyms:** Certainty, fatality
**Antonyms:** Avoidability
**Example:** The ineluctability of death is a core theme of Stoic philosophy.

**Option 2: Avoidability** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** The quality of being avoidable.
**Hindi Meaning:** टालने योग्यता
**Synonyms:** Possibility, prevention
**Antonyms:** Ineluctability
**Example:** The ineluctability of death is a core theme of Stoic philosophy.

**Option 3: Fatality**
**English Meaning:** The quality of causing death or being inevitable.
**Hindi Meaning:** घातकता
**Synonyms:** Inevitability
**Antonyms:** Avoidability
**Example:** The fatality of the disease shocked everyone.

**Option 4: Unavoidability**
**English Meaning:** The quality of being impossible to avoid.
**Hindi Meaning:** अपरिहार्यता
**Synonyms:** Inevitability
**Antonyms:** Avoidability
**Example:** The unavoidability of the change was accepted.

The question asks for the antonym. 'Avoidability' is the opposite of 'Ineluctability'.

questions.text: Choose the word that best fits the blank: The biopic presents the athlete's life as an _____ of perseverance.
questions.solution:
Question word: **apotheosis** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** The highest point of glory; elevation to divine status.
**Hindi Meaning:** महिमामंडन, आदर्श रूप
**Synonyms:** Glorification, deification, idealization
**Antonyms:** Humiliation, degradation, disgrace
**Example:** The biopic presents the athlete’s life as an apotheosis of perseverance.

**Option 0: humiliation**
**English Meaning:** The state of being humiliated.
**Hindi Meaning:** अपमान
**Synonyms:** Degradation, disgrace
**Antonyms:** Apotheosis
**Example:** The defeat brought humiliation.

**Option 1: degradation**
**English Meaning:** The process of becoming worse.
**Hindi Meaning:** अवनति
**Synonyms:** Humiliation, disgrace
**Antonyms:** Apotheosis
**Example:** The scandal led to his degradation.

**Option 2: apotheosis** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** The highest point of glory; elevation to divine status.
**Hindi Meaning:** महिमामंडन, आदर्श रूप
**Synonyms:** Glorification, deification
**Antonyms:** Humiliation, degradation
**Example:** The biopic presents the athlete’s life as an apotheosis of perseverance.

**Option 3: disgrace**
**English Meaning:** Loss of reputation.
**Hindi Meaning:** बदनामी
**Synonyms:** Humiliation, degradation
**Antonyms:** Apotheosis
**Example:** The scandal brought disgrace to the family.

**Option 4: debasement**
**English Meaning:** The act of lowering in quality.
**Hindi Meaning:** गिरावट
**Synonyms:** Degradation
**Antonyms:** Apotheosis
**Example:** The debasement of moral values is worrying.

The sentence shows the highest glorification of perseverance. Only 'apotheosis' fits.

questions.text: One-word substitute: One who marries while already married.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Bigamist** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** One who marries while already married.
**Hindi Meaning:** द्विविवाह करने वाला
**Example:** He was punished for bigamy.

**Option 0: Philogynist**
**English Meaning:** One who loves women.
**Hindi Meaning:** स्त्रीप्रेमी
**Example:** He was a philogynist admired by all.

**Option 1: Bibliophile**
**English Meaning:** One who loves books.
**Hindi Meaning:** पुस्तकप्रेमी
**Example:** The bibliophile spent hours in libraries.

**Option 2: Bigamist** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** One who marries while already married.
**Hindi Meaning:** द्विविवाह करने वाला
**Example:** He was punished for bigamy.

**Option 3: Bibliophobe**
**English Meaning:** One who hates books.
**Hindi Meaning:** पुस्तकद्वेषी
**Example:** The bibliophobe disliked reading.

**Option 4: Fatalist**
**English Meaning:** A person who believes in fate.
**Hindi Meaning:** भाग्यवादी
**Example:** Being a fatalist, he accepted the result without anger.

The exact one-word substitute for 'One who marries while already married' is 'Bigamist'.

Question word: **Philogynist** (the correct fit)

**Option 4: Stoic**

The exact one-word substitute for 'One who loves women' is 'Philogynist'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the phrasal verb 'Bring under':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Bring under** (the correct meaning is 'Subdue / control')

**English Meaning:** Subdue / control.
**Hindi Meaning:** नियंत्रण में लाना
**Example:** The rebels were brought under control.

**Option 0: Take a shortcut**
**English Meaning:** Affect all equally / take a shortcut.
**Hindi Meaning:** सीधा रास्ता लेना
**Example:** The issue cuts across party lines.

**Option 1: Rehearse quickly**
**English Meaning:** Rehearse / squander quickly.
**Hindi Meaning:** दोहराना
**Example:** Let’s run through the notes again.

**Option 2: Subdue / control** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Subdue / control.
**Hindi Meaning:** नियंत्रण में लाना
**Example:** The rebels were brought under control.

**Option 3: Resemble**
**English Meaning:** Resemble.
**Hindi Meaning:** जैसा होना
**Example:** She takes after her mother.

**Option 4: Set aside time**
**English Meaning:** To set aside time for something.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय निकालना
**Example:** You must make time for exercise.

The phrasal verb 'Bring under' means to bring under control. The correct option is 'Subdue / control'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the phrasal verb 'Cut across':
options: Affect all equally / take a shortcut
questions.solution:
Question word: **Cut across** (the correct meaning is 'Affect all equally / take a shortcut')

**English Meaning:** Affect all equally / take a shortcut.
**Hindi Meaning:** सभी को प्रभावित करना / सीधा रास्ता लेना
**Example:** The issue cuts across party lines.

**Option 0: Subdue / control**
**English Meaning:** Subdue / control.
**Hindi Meaning:** नियंत्रण में लाना
**Example:** The rebels were brought under control.

**Option 1: Rehearse quickly**
**English Meaning:** Rehearse / squander quickly.
**Hindi Meaning:** दोहराना
**Example:** Let’s run through the notes again.

**Option 2: Affect all equally / take a shortcut** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Affect all equally / take a shortcut.
**Hindi Meaning:** सभी को प्रभावित करना / सीधा रास्ता लेना
**Example:** The issue cuts across party lines.

**Option 3: Resemble**
**English Meaning:** Resemble.
**Hindi Meaning:** जैसा होना
**Example:** She takes after her mother.

**Option 4: Set aside time**
**English Meaning:** To set aside time for something.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय निकालना
**Example:** You must make time for exercise.

The phrasal verb 'Cut across' means to affect everyone the same way or take a shortcut. The correct option is 'Affect all equally / take a shortcut'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom 'Ahead of time':
options: To complete something before the expected time
questions.solution:
Question word: **Ahead of time** (the correct meaning is 'To complete something before the expected time')

**English Meaning:** To complete something before the expected time.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय से पहले पूरा करना
**Example:** She finished the assignment ahead of time.

**Option 0: Just in time**
**English Meaning:** Just in time, at the last possible moment.
**Hindi Meaning:** ठीक समय पर
**Example:** The doctor arrived in the nick of time.

**Option 1: Time passes quickly**
**English Meaning:** Time passes very quickly.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय उड़ता है
**Example:** Time flies when you are having fun.

**Option 2: To complete something before the expected time** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** To complete something before the expected time.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय से पहले पूरा करना
**Example:** She finished the assignment ahead of time.

**Option 3: Time is valuable**
**English Meaning:** Time is valuable, and should not be wasted.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय मूल्यवान है
**Example:** Don’t waste time – time is money.

**Option 4: To set aside time**
**English Meaning:** To set aside time for something.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय निकालना
**Example:** You must make time for exercise.

The idiom 'Ahead of time' means finishing early. The correct option is 'To complete something before the expected time'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom 'In the nick of time':
options: To complete something before the expected time
Just in time, at the last possible moment
questions.solution:
Question word: **In the nick of time** (the correct meaning is 'Just in time, at the last possible moment')

**English Meaning:** Just in time, at the last possible moment.
**Hindi Meaning:** आखिरी पल में
**Example:** The doctor arrived in the nick of time.

**Option 0: To complete something before the expected time**
**English Meaning:** To complete something before the expected time.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय से पहले पूरा करना
**Example:** She finished the assignment ahead of time.

**Option 1: Time passes quickly**
**English Meaning:** Time passes very quickly.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय उड़ता है
**Example:** Time flies when you are having fun.

**Option 2: Just in time, at the last possible moment** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Just in time, at the last possible moment.
**Hindi Meaning:** आखिरी पल में
**Example:** The doctor arrived in the nick of time.

**Option 3: Time is valuable**
**English Meaning:** Time is valuable, and should not be wasted.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय मूल्यवान है
**Example:** Don’t waste time – time is money.

**Option 4: To set aside time**
**English Meaning:** To set aside time for something.
**Hindi Meaning:** समय निकालना
**Example:** You must make time for exercise.

The idiom 'In the nick of time' means arriving at the very last moment to prevent disaster. The correct option is 'Just in time, at the last possible moment'.
